Total Privacy · Our Story

A Sanctuary
in the Taita Hills

Nestled within the ancient forests of the Taita Hills in southeastern Kenya, Chawia Luxury Camp offers an intimate escape where raw wilderness meets thoughtful comfort.

The Taita Hills are one of East Africa's most biodiverse regions — a fragmented Afromontane forest teeming with endemic birds, rare wildlife, and landscapes that shift from mist-draped peaks to sun-drenched valleys.

We believe in travel that lingers. Just the two of you — no other guests, no shared spaces, no interruptions. Wake to out-of-this-world sunrises above the Sagalla Hills and Chawia Hills. Afternoons swimming in your private natural pool, carved by the seasonal river that flows through the property. Evenings by the fire as the forest settles into night. At Chawia, total privacy is not a feature — it is the entire philosophy.

The Estate

One Cottage.
Total Privacy.

What Awaits You

Curated
Experiences

From guided art sessions with Imara-Bella Thorpe to full-day safaris in Tsavo, every experience at Chawia is designed to be entirely yours.

01
🎨

Paint & Sip
with Imara-Bella Thorpe

An intimate creative session hosted by artist Imara-Bella Thorpe. Uncork a glass of wine, pick up a brush, and let the colours of the Taita Hills inspire your canvas. No experience needed — just curiosity and good company.

Signature Experience
02
🎨

Paint & Sip
with Achieng Owira

An intimate session with artist and storyteller Achieng Owira. As your brush moves across the canvas, Achieng weaves stories — of the Taita Hills, its people, its spirits, and its landscape. You leave with more than a painting; you leave with a piece of this place and its soul. Drinks, all materials, and canvas included.

Guided · Artist Session
03
🖼️

Paint & Sip

A self-directed creative session — your canvas, your vision, your pace. Set up on the rocks with your favourite sip in hand and let the landscape inspire you. No instruction, no rules. Just you, the forest, and a blank canvas.

Creative · Unguided
09
🦁

Full Day Safari
Game Drive

Chawia sits at the gateway to two of Kenya's most legendary wilderness areas — and both are within striking distance for an unforgettable full day out.

Tsavo West

Journey into Tsavo West and discover one of East Africa's most dramatic landscapes. Walk the eerie, otherworldly Shetani Lava Flow — a vast field of black volcanic rock that erupted just 200 years ago and still feels like the edge of the earth. Drift above crystal-clear underwater springs at Mzima Springs, where hippos and crocodiles glide beneath the surface and you watch from a submerged viewing chamber. Then ascend to Poachers Lookout for a panoramic view across the park that stretches to Kilimanjaro on a clear day.

Tsavo East

Cross into Tsavo East — one of the world's largest national parks — and encounter the famous red elephants, their hides dusted rust-red from the iconic laterite soil. Vast open plains make for exceptional game viewing: lion, leopard, buffalo, and the great herds that have roamed this landscape for centuries. The Galana River cuts through the park, drawing wildlife to its banks in spectacular concentrations.

Full Day · Tsavo West & East
04
🏹

Archery

Find your focus in the fresh forest air. Our archery range is a surprisingly meditative experience — each arrow a lesson in stillness, breath, and precision. Suitable for all levels, from first-timers to those looking to sharpen their aim.

Activity · All Levels
05
🌱

Tree Planting

Leave Chawia greener than you found it. Through our partnership with Kenya Forest Service Service, we plant 10 trees for every guest stay — your visit actively restores one of East Africa's most precious ecosystems. You're also welcome to get your hands in the soil and plant one yourself, a living legacy tagged with your name that will outlast your stay by generations.

Conservation · Kenya Forest Service Partnership
07
🐴

Horse Riding
Safari

See the Taita Hills from the saddle — the most intimate way to move through a landscape. Our horse riding safaris take you through forest trails, open hillsides, and viewpoints that no vehicle can reach. Suitable for all riding levels.

Safari · All Levels
08
🏠

Bring Your
Own Horse

Chawia Luxury Camp welcomes your horses. Our intimate stabling accommodates exactly 2 horses — just as the cottage is yours alone, the stables are theirs. Arrive together, ride together, rest together — the ultimate equestrian retreat.

Equestrian · Stabling Available

The Cottage

Your Private
Retreat

Exclusively yours. Our one-bedroom cottage welcomes a maximum of two guests — and when you are here, the entire property is yours alone. No other guests. No shared paths. No chance encounters. Just you, the forest, and total, uncompromising privacy.

Wake to a sunrise that sets the whole sky on fire. On cooler evenings, the cottage fireplace fills the room with warmth as the forest settles into night. Your dedicated butler anticipates every need — from arranging experiences to ensuring your cottage is perfect at every moment. Take a morning dip in your private natural swimming spot, carved by the seasonal river that runs through the property. Dine alfresco on your private stone terrace — a hand-laid stage in the forest, lit by lanterns, cast iron on the grill, your butler close at hand. Every meal is pre-selected before arrival, freshly prepared, nothing wasted. End the evening by the fireplace with a cigar from the in-cottage humidor as the forest falls silent around you.

The Bedroom

Sleep beneath
bamboo & stars

The Falls

Signature Feature

A Waterfall
All Your Own

On the grounds of Chawia Luxury Camp, a natural waterfall cascades through layered rock and ancient forest. One of those rare things — a wild place that feels like it was made just for you.

Slip into the natural swimming spot carved by the seasonal river — cool, clear water, ancient rock, and total solitude. Sit on the rocks and listen. Dine beside the falls as lanterns flicker through the trees. Let the sound of falling water dissolve whatever you brought with you from the city.

Beyond the Camp

Taita Hills Excursions

🏔️

Kenyatta Caves Mboga / Mchoo

40–50 min drive·Easy to Moderate·History & Independence

The Experience

A major historical site used as a hideout by Jomo Kenyatta and local freedom fighters during Kenya's struggle for independence. The caves were strategic for resistance, storage, planning, and refuge. Explore the chambers, natural rock formations, and ventilation points with a guided heritage story from a local custodian. Views over Wundanyi Valley included.

Wildlife & Highlights

Birds: Taita White-eye, Taita Apalis, Tacazze Sunbird. Trees & flora: Podocarpus, African Olive, wild figs. Rich butterfly activity during warm months. An optional short hike through indigenous forest completes the visit.

🪨

Wesu Rock & Viewpoint

30 min drive·Easy·Viewpoint & Photography

The Experience

One of the most dramatic viewpoints in all of Taita. Sheer rocky outcrops drop into deep valleys with panoramic views of Wundanyi, Mwatate, and the surrounding hills. A superb spot for sunrise and sunset photography, with cool mountain breezes and excellent storytelling about Taita culture, clans, agriculture, and geology.

Wildlife & Highlights

Rock Hyrax seen occasionally. Birds: Augur Buzzard, Verreaux's Eagle, swifts riding the thermals. In early morning, mist forms dramatic cloud seas across the valleys below — a photographer's dream.

🌿

Ngangao Forest Reserve

30 min drive·Easy to Moderate·Cloud Forest & Endemics

The Experience

One of the last remaining patches of indigenous cloud forest in the Taita Hills — cool, moist, and utterly tranquil. Well-marked trails wind through a magical forest canopy draped in moss, orchids, and vines. Ideal for nature lovers, picnics in a forest clearing, and learning about water catchments, conservation, and traditional plant use with a nature guide.

Endemic Species

Taita Thrush (Critically Endangered), Taita Apalis and Taita White-eye — found nowhere else on Earth. Forest butterflies, chameleons, and tree frogs. Between Wundanyi and Chawia, just 30 minutes away.

⛰️

Vuria Peak Highest Point in Taita — 2,228m

45–60 min drive·Moderate to Hard·Epic Views & Sacred Land

The Experience

The highest point in the Taita Hills commands a full 360° panorama. On a clear day, sightings include the Tsavo plains, Taveta, Lakes Chala and Jipe, and Mount Kilimanjaro rising above the clouds. A steep but deeply rewarding hike through highland grasslands and scree slopes. Vuria is a sacred peak for local communities, rich with cultural legends about spirits, warriors, and sacred rites.

Wildlife & Highlights

Vulturine Guinea fowl in foothills, eagles on thermals. Wild orchids and medicinal plants, hyrax and bushbuck occasionally. When clouds settle below the summit, it feels like standing above the world itself.

🌳

Iyale Hill & Forest

20 min drive·Easy·Woodland, Birds & Family

The Experience

A beautiful blend of indigenous forest and open viewpoints rising above Wundanyi. Good trails make for easy, shaded walking — perfect for families, relaxed nature lovers, birdwatchers, and botanists. Scenic views toward Wundanyi and Mwanda. A wonderful picnic and photography spot just 20 minutes from the camp.

Endemic Species

Taita White-eye, Green-headed Oriole, and many sunbirds. Indigenous Camphor trees, wild banana, giant ferns. Butterfly corridors alive during warm seasons.

Excursion Drive from Chawia Main Interest Difficulty
Kenyatta Caves40–50 minHistory, Independence, BirdsEasy to Moderate
Wesu Rock30 minViewpoint, Photography, CultureEasy
Ngangao Forest30 minCloud Forest, Endemics, PicnicEasy to Moderate
Vuria Peak45–60 minHighest Peak, Epic Views, KilimanjaroModerate to Hard
Iyale Hill20 minWoodland, Birds, Family VisitEasy

À La Carte

The Chawia Menu

Our menu is chosen before your arrival — ensuring every dish is prepared fresh, nothing goes to waste, and your table is set exactly as you imagined. Ingredients are locally sourced where possible, celebrating the flavours of coastal and highland Kenya.

Breakfast

Taita Hills Sunrise Platter

Farm eggs your way, grilled tomatoes, sautéed mushrooms, avocado, and sourdough toast with local honey

Swahili Omelette

Spiced eggs with pilipili, onion, coriander, and coastal-style tomato chutney

Fresh Fruit Platter

Seasonal tropical fruits — mango, pawpaw, pineapple, passion fruit — with natural yoghurt and wildflower honey

Mandazi & Chai

Hand-rolled Kenyan doughnuts with spiced masala tea or freshly brewed Kenyan coffee

Light Bites & Starters

Avocado & Crab Tartine

Coastal crab on toasted sourdough with avocado, lime, chilli oil, and micro herbs

Grilled Maize & Coconut Soup

Sweet roasted maize blended with coconut milk, ginger, and a drizzle of chilli-infused oil

Taita Charcuterie Board

Cured meats, local cheeses, roasted nuts, dried fruits, and artisan crackers with fig preserve

Mains

Fillet Mignon — Red Wine Reduction

Prime beef tenderloin, pan-seared to perfection and finished with a rich Cabernet Sauvignon reduction, roasted garlic butter, wilted spinach, and truffled potato gratin

Seafood Platter

A coastal celebration — tiger prawns, calamari, and fresh fish of the day, grilled over open flame with saffron butter, charred lemon, and a herb-dressed green salad

Prawn & Lobster Bisque Linguine

Freshwater prawns and lobster tail in a velvety bisque sauce tossed with linguine, cherry tomatoes, fresh basil, and a splash of white wine

Slow-Braised Lamb Shank

Tamarind and pilipili-rubbed lamb, slow-cooked over coals, served with coconut ugali and braised greens

Forest Mushroom Risotto

Wild mushrooms foraged from the Taita Hills, arborio rice, parmesan, truffle oil, and fresh herbs — a vegetarian signature

Nyama Choma Platter

Charcoal-grilled beef and chicken, served with roasted vegetables, ugali, sukuma wiki, and house-made chilli sauce

Desserts

Passion Fruit Panna Cotta

Silky vanilla panna cotta with a tangy passion fruit coulis and toasted coconut flakes

Dark Chocolate & Cardamom Pot

Warm Kenyan dark chocolate with cardamom cream and crushed macadamia nuts

Taita Honey Cake

Light sponge soaked in local wildflower honey, served with crème fraîche and seasonal berries

Drinks

Non-Alcoholic

Freshly squeezed juices · Taita wildflower lemonade · Masala chai · Kenyan coffee · Hibiscus iced tea · Still & sparkling water

Wines & Spirits

Bottle service only. Bottle service only · Curated selection of wines · Craft gin & tonics · Sundowner cocktails · Local Kenyan craft beer · Champagne on request

The Chawia Cocktail

Our signature drink — hibiscus, lime, ginger, and a measure of your choice. Served in recycled Kitengela glass over hand-chipped ice.

Menu selections are made prior to your arrival. Please share any dietary requirements, allergies, or preferences in your booking form — we'll tailor every dish to you. All alcohol is bottle service only — please select your preferred wines and spirits at booking.

Our Commitment

Luxury That Gives Back

🌳

10 Trees Per Stay

Every booking triggers a planting. In partnership with Kenya Forest Service Service, we restore the Taita Hills forest one guest at a time.

🚫

Completely Plastic-Free

No single-use plastics anywhere on the property. We believe the wilderness deserves better than waste.

🍶

Recycled Glass Tableware

All glassware and cutlery handcrafted from recycled glass, sourced from Kitengela Glass and Green Thing Kenya.

☀️

Solar-Powered Water

Our water is pumped entirely by solar energy — clean, quiet, and off-grid. No generators, no fossil fuels.

🍽️

Zero-Waste À La Carte

Bite-sized portions and pre-selected menus mean every ingredient is sourced fresh. Nothing goes to waste.

⚡

Green Transport

We use eBee electric bikes and horses for tours — zero emissions, zero noise. The land stays exactly as you found it.

📵

Zero Paper Use

Fully digital — from booking to menu to check-out. No printed materials, no waste. Every process is paperless.

🛒

Local Sourcing

All supplies sourced from local community farmers and producers. Your stay directly supports the livelihoods of families in the Taita Hills.

📚

Education Sponsorship

We sponsor education for children in the surrounding community — because luxury travel should lift those who share this land with us.
